result:
Done. I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.cs`, and the project builds cleanly with no warnings.

- `EscapeField` returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- `JoinRow` escapes each field and joins with commas; the empty list yields the empty string.
- `SplitRow` is a single-pass parser: a field starting with `"` is read until the un-doubled closing quote (doubled quotes become one literal quote, commas/CR/LF inside are content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. A trailing comma produces a final empty field, and the empty line parses to one empty field.

`NeedsQuoting` is untouched. I also verified behavior at runtime in a throwaway console project (deleted afterward, source tree untouched): round-trips for plain fields, fields with commas/quotes/CRLF, empty and all-empty fields, and the two edge cases from the spec — `JoinRow([])` is `""` and `SplitRow("")` is a single empty field — all passed.
